title: Breitmann As An Uhlan I. The Vision.
content:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Bjן¿½ foeri ek thן¿½r,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Brynthings apaldr!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Magni blandinn
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ok magentן¿½ri,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Fullr er hann ljoda.ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ - Sigrdrן¿½furnן¿½l

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Beer I bear to thee,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Battleן¿½s great apple-tree!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Mingled with might
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And with bright glory,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ All full of song.ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ - The Edda.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Dere vas vonce oopon a dimes a Frantchman who asket if a Sharman could hafe ן¿½sprit. Allowin for his pad shbellin, de reater will find dat der Herr Breitmann was hafe a spree goot many dimes. You gant ged rount de Dootch.ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ - Fritz Swackenhammer.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ GOTTS blitz! blau Feuer, potz bomben Tod!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vot shimmers in de mitnacht roth?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Like hell-shtrom boorst oן¿½er heafenן¿½s plain,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Trowin dead light on eart acain:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ja! wide im nord om Odin shtone
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Lies a shiant form im glare alone.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Troonk py de eis-kalt roarin shdream
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der Hans ish hafe ein wunder tream.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Troonk om haunted Odinstein
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Im Hexenlicht und Elfenschein
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhere blooty Druids omens trew
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ From grin und screech of shaps dey slew;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Or vhere der Norseman long of yore
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vas carven eagles on de shore,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ As oן¿½er him yell de Valkyr broot
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und crows valk round knee teep im ploot,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhile rabens schkreem oן¿½er ruddy bay;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dere ten pottles troonk Hans Breitmann lay.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Fast und rof der war-man shnore
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Like de hammer-shlog of Thor,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Schnell ash Mjן¿½llnerן¿½s bang und beat
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Heaved de form from het to veet
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhile apofe him in de shkies
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dere he saw a glorie rise,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und im mittle von it all
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De iron lords of crate Valhall.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Long he gaze mit wן¿½lfen glare
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ At de Aesir in de air,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Long mit schneerin bן¿½ren grin
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He toorn his nase auf und hin
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ (For neן¿½er a Sherman tam de otts
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vas efer yet gife in to Gotts),
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dill avery Aes owned oop dat he
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A gott-like man of brass moost pe.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Shtern der Breitmann raise his het,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To his fader Gotts he set:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Let your worts of wisehood shlip;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Rush your runes, und let ן¿½em rip!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For you de gotts hafe efer pe
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Of dose who vere ash gotts to me:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Alt Thor der Thן¿½ren here pelow
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vot hell you vants, Iן¿½d like to know?ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Antworded ash de donner clangs,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der fader of de iron bangs:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½De gotts will let de hell-dogs go,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und raise damnation here pelow;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Until de sassy Frenchmen schmell
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De rifers ten dat roon troo hell
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To telle dis I comme dence,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dou lord of lion impudence.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Drafeller! I know dee vell!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Breitmann improturbable!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhen on eart I hat my shy,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Breitmann of dat age vas I.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I schwear py Thor! so crate und gay,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I smashed de Jן¿½tuns in my tay,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und dou shall pe ge-writ sooplime
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ash de crate Thor of deiner time.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Now ve lets de eagles vly
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Skreemin troo de vlamin shky,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Our own specials: dare nod laugh;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For in de London Telegraph,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A voondrous poy vot make oos shdare,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For hop vhat may, heן¿½s alvays dere!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vill dell de worlt, troo blut and flame,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Hans Breitmann ist der Uhlanן¿½s name.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Und all dou eן¿½er on eart has done,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ From oop gang oontil settin sun,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vill pe ash nix I schvear py Thor!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To vat douן¿½lt do in dieser war;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Plazin roofs und mordered men,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Hell set loose on eart again;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Rush und ride in shtorm und floot,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Cannon roarin, pools of bloot;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Deutschland mad in fool career,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Led py dy Uhlanen speer,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Hellן¿½s harfest sheafs of fictorie,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Reaped mit deatן¿½s sword und reapt by dee!

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Ja! On many a dorf und disch,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dou shalt pring a requisish;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Dwendy dimes de Frן¿½ntscher men
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Hafe sporned dy land in blut acain-
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ All dose dwenty dimes in von,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Py Deutschland shall to France pe done,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und dwenty dimes in blut and wein
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Shalst dou refenge de Palatine.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Go! mit shpeer und fiery muth!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Go! mit durst for bier und blut!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Go! mit lofe for Vaterland,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Into burning fury fanned:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Towns und hen-roosts shall hafe shown
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vhere der Uhlan ist peen gone,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und cocks vill roon und men crow tame
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To hear of der Uhlanen name.ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Der fision fadet in de shky,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und hours vent on und time goed py.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Vot heardest dou, Napolium?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De rumpitty, rumpitty, rumpitty poom!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ven you hear de sound of de droom,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Oh denn you know dat de Dootch hafe coom,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De treadful roarin Dootch, mit de droom
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Und de roompitty, pumpitty, poompity pum!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ De wild ferocious Dootch on a bum,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Mit cannon roar und pattle hum,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Mit fee und faw on de foe und fum!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Led py de awful Breitemum!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Bitty boom!! BOOM!!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Charles G. Leland represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Charles G. Leland's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
